channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Connor Ingram Secures First Playoff Win for Edmonton Oilers
item.description: Edmonton Oilers goaltender Connor Ingram recorded his first career playoff victory in a tightly contested 4-3 win against the Anaheim Ducks, opening the first round of the NHL playoffs. The 30-year-ol...

item.title: UK unemployment rate unexpectedly decreases to 4.9%
item.description: The UK has seen an unexpected decline in its unemployment rate, dropping to 4.9%, according to recent government statistics. This decrease comes at a time when economists had predicted either a stable...

item.title: Policemen and Families in Lagos Iponri Barracks Face Eviction Amid Housing Crisis
item.description: Tension has risen at Iponri Police Barracks in Lagos as hundreds of police officers and their families are facing eviction. Authorities have ordered them to vacate their apartments by April 24th and 2...

item.title: Ruto and Italy’s Mattarella Hold Talks in Rome to Boost Trade and Cooperation
item.description: President William Ruto held a high-level meeting with Italy’s President Sergio Mattarella in Rome on 20 April 2026, in a move aimed at strengthening bilateral ties between Kenya and Italy. The discuss...

item.title: Jollibee opens first LEED-certified quick service restaurant in Tuguegarao
item.description: Jollibee Philippines has marked a significant step in its sustainability efforts with the opening of the country’s first quick service restaurant (QSR) to receive LEED O+M (Operations and Maintenance)...

item.title: 17-year-old charged with arson at Kenton United Synagogue in Harrow
item.description: A 17-year-old boy from north-west London has been formally charged with arson following an incident at Kenton United Synagogue in Harrow, according to the Metropolitan Police. The teenager, who reside...

item.title: 19 Suspected Rebels Killed in Negros Occidental Clash, Displacing Hundreds
item.description: Nineteen suspected members of the New People’s Army (NPA) were killed in a prolonged encounter with government forces in Sitio Sinugmawan, Barangay Salamanca, Toboso, Negros Occidental on April 19, 20...

item.title: Cuddalore and Puducherry officials meet to strengthen border surveillance before Tamil Nadu Assembly Elections
item.description: In preparation for the upcoming Tamil Nadu Assembly elections scheduled on April 23, 2026, officials from Cuddalore district in Tamil Nadu and the Union Territory of Puducherry held a joint inter-Stat...

item.title: Authorities Seize Counterfeit Fertiliser and Expose Supply Chain Fraud in Kakamega
item.description: Authorities in Kenya have recovered 250 bags of suspected counterfeit fertiliser during a coordinated operation in Kakamega County, highlighting a growing threat to the country’s agricultural sector. ...

item.title: Doctor killed in suspected targeted attack near Mehran Hotel in Karachi
item.description: A young doctor, Dr Sarang Memon, was shot dead in what police believe to be a targeted attack near Karachi’s Mehran Hotel late Monday night. According to DIG South Syed Asad Raza, the victim was trave...

item.title: How Jaime and Marcos Jaquez built their athletic careers alongside sister Gabriela
item.description: Gabriela Jaquez and her brothers, Jaime and Marcos Jaquez, were brought up in a very competitive and sports-driven family in Camarillo, California. Their parents, both former college basketball player...

item.title: Stephen Colbert Responds to Allegations of Excessive Drinking Against FBI Director Kash Patel
item.description: Stephen Colbert mocked FBI Director Kash Patel on his show after The Atlantic accused Patel of excessive drinking and being unreachable during a critical moment. The report claimed that Patel was behi...

item.title: Kerala Vigilance Exposes Bribery in Illegal Soil Mining Scam
item.description: In a major crackdown on illegal soil mining and transportation, Kerala's Vigilance Department has uncovered a massive bribery scandal involving government officials. A probe revealed that 14 officials...

item.title: Apple Announces Tim Cook Succession Plan as John Ternus Set to Become New CEO in September
item.description: Apple don announce big news say their longtime CEO, Tim Cook, go step down from the top position for September 2026. The 65-year-old boss wey don lead the company since 2011 after Steve Jobs, go now b...

item.title: Early screening and lifestyle changes key to preventing silent heart disease
item.description: Heart disease remains one of the leading causes of serious illness and death, largely because it can develop quietly over many years without noticeable symptoms. According to pharmacist Thabo Lephalal...

item.title: Iran Denounces U.S. Navy Seizure of Iranian Cargo Ship Near Strait of Hormuz as Piracy
item.description: Iran has sharply condemned the U.S. Navy’s seizure of an Iranian‑flagged cargo vessel in the Strait of Hormuz, accusing Washington of committing an act of piracy and violating a fragile ceasefire. Acc...

item.title: Brisbane Broncos rely on rookies to fill injury-hit squad
item.description: The Brisbane Broncos are facing a significant injury crisis, with over $5 million worth of players sidelined, including key forwards Payne Haas and Corey Jensen. Despite this, captain Adam Reynolds re...

item.title: Sysco expands food waste reduction and sustainability efforts across Canada
item.description: Sysco Canada Inc. continues to strengthen its commitment to food sustainability and security through expanded waste diversion and community support initiatives. Building on a long-standing program tha...

item.title: Stellenbosch University team shows electric motorbikes can thrive in Africa with solar power
item.description: A team from Stellenbosch University has proven that electric motorbikes, powered by solar energy, can thrive in real African conditions, with a 6000km journey from Nairobi to Stellenbosch. This journe...

item.title: Courses Nigerians Can Study to Improve Chances of Working or Earning Abroad
item.description: This article breaks down practical courses Nigerians fit study if dem dey plan either to relocate abroad or earn foreign income from Nigeria. E first explain say “working abroad” no be only travelling...
